# Reliability Centered Theory of Knowledge

**Node Type:** synthesis
**Node ID:** fb64d467-b501-4566-8e42-a037b88aed72
**Depth:** 5
**Terminal:** No

**Parent:** [Do cases of accidental truth challenge functionalism?](do-cases-of-accidental-truth-challenge-functionalism-antithesis-5768dcae-4fa9-413b-b1ef-c9a4795b02f8.md)

## Content

**A person knows a proposition if their belief is generated by a process that reliably produces true beliefs**, **Reliability of the process is necessary for a belief to count as knowledge**, **The process must function according to a design plan that generally promotes truth**
